methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Revere is a stealth-stage Defense & Security company founded by Max Susman and Bobby Collins. The valuation exercise draws from a 10-company comp universe spanning the Defense & Security sector: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable data at the stealth stage. Within the 21 same-sector peers identified, zero companies matched both Revere's stage and valuation tier simultaneously, falling below the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. A fallback to all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias and obscure stage-specific risk factors material to early-stage Defense & Security valuations.

This analysis is therefore constrained: no triangulated valuation range can be supported without either (1) direct transaction data for same-stage Defense & Security companies, (2) founder/investor guidance on capital efficiency metrics, or (3) disclosed financials from the named private peers. The primary uncertainty is the absence of narrow-set comparables, which typically anchor early-stage valuations in capital-efficient or mission-critical sectors. Further diligence should prioritize obtaining Series A or seed-round data from the top five private peers to establish defensible benchmarks.
